title: Glazed Brown Sugar American Shrimp Bowl
description: A mustardy, juicy, and energizing American dish featuring brown sugar, tandoor, and pescatarian-friendly ingredients, served in a Korean brass bowl.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together brown sugar, whole grain mustard, tandoor seasoning, salt, and black pepper.
Add the large shrimp to the bowl and toss to coat with the brown sugar mixture.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, until pink and cooked through.
Transfer the shrimp to a baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven for 5 minutes.
While the shrimp are baking, prepare the Korean brass bowls by placing a bed of chopped fresh parsley in the bottom of each bowl.
Once the shrimp are done, remove them from the oven and place them on top of the parsley in the bowls.
Drizzle any remaining brown sugar glaze from the skillet over the shrimp and serve immediately.
